# Code of Conduct

The Binary Labs is committed to a respectful, welcoming, and constructive open-source community.

## Our expectations

We ask participants to:

- communicate with respect
- assume good intent and discuss disagreements in good faith
- focus feedback on ideas, code, and behavior, not people
- help make collaboration accessible to contributors with different backgrounds and experience levels

## Unacceptable behavior

The following behavior is not acceptable:

- harassment or personal attacks
- discrimination or exclusionary language
- intimidation, threats, or sustained hostility
- deliberate disruption of community discussions or contribution processes
- sharing private information without consent

## Scope

This code of conduct applies in organization repositories, issues, pull requests, discussions, and other public collaboration spaces operated by The Binary Labs.

## Enforcement

Maintainers may remove, edit, or reject contributions and comments that violate this code of conduct. Repeated or severe violations may lead to temporary or permanent restrictions from participation.

## Reporting

If you experience or witness unacceptable behavior, contact the maintainers privately through the channels defined by the relevant repository or organization.
